'''Ryan Bailey''' (born 11 November 1983) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}}. A [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] international and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] representative, he played the majority of his club career at the [[Leeds Rhinos]], making over 300 appearances and winning six [[Super League]] championships and three [[World Club Challenge]]s with the club. After leaving Leeds, Bailey played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]]. After Super League his playing career continued in League 1 with a Canadian club, [[Toronto Wolfpack]], and then at [[Workington Town]]. He then went to [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Bailey was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. His father is of West Indian heritage.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=25817 |title=Leeds Rhinos back rugby league in Jamacia [sic] |access-date=11 March 2009 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060709075854/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=25817 |archive-date=9 July 2006 |df=dmy-all }}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
Bailey made his [[Super League]] début for Leeds in 2002 at age 18.<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/jul/15/leedsrhinos-rugbyleague |title=Ryan Bailey turns down move to Australia to stay at Leeds Rhinos |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=15 July 2013 |access-date=12 February 2019}}</ref>

Bailey played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] as a {{rlp|pr}} in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Bradford Bulls]]. He was then selected in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain team]] to compete in the end of season [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] Bailey played from the interchange bench in the Lions' 44–4 loss.

As [[Super League IX]] champions, the Leeds club faced [[2004 NRL season]] premiers, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[2005 World Club Challenge]]. Bailey played as a {{rlp|pr}} in Leeds' 39–32 victory. He played for Leeds in the [[2005 Challenge Cup]] Final as a {{rlp|pr}} in their loss against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull stun Leeds in Challenge Cup|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/4169230.stm|access-date=17 August 2013|newspaper=[[BBC News]]|date=27 August 2005}}</ref> Bailey played for the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club as a {{rlp|pr}} in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]].  Bailey played in the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=4 October 2008|access-date=5 October 2008}}</ref>

Bailey played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref> Bailey played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref> Bailey played from the interchange bench for Leeds in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

Bailey played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>

[[File:Ryan Bailey (rugby league).jpg|thumb|250px|right|Bailey (pictured right) playing for the Leeds Rhinos in 2012.]]

Bailey played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

Bailey played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>
In 2014, Leeds held a testimonial game for Bailey against [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Leeds 62 London Broncos 10|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/news/article/7418/9144622/leeds-62-london-broncos-10|website=Sporting Life|access-date=18 January 2015|date=31 January 2014}}</ref> On 8 May 2014, he made his 300th career appearance for the Leeds club. Bailey played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

===Hull KR===
In 2015 Bailey moved to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. He was not fit enough to play his former club [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the opening game of the season but went on to score on his début the week after. In March it was announced Bailey had been released because of personal issues after only playing one game for the club.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Castleford Tigers===
A month later he joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] for the remainder of the season where he rejoined former coach [[Daryl Powell]]. He played six games and scored one try before being released at the end of the season.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Warrington Wolves===
In November 2015 [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] announced the signing of Bailey on a one-year contract for the 2016 season. There, he would play under [[Tony Smith (rugby league born 1967)|Tony Smith]] again with whom he won two Grand Finals with at Leeds. Bailey publicly opened up about his struggle with depression in 2016.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35354450|title=Ryan Bailey: Help for depression aided Warrington move|date=19 January 2016|work=BBC Sport|access-date=14 March 2023}}</ref>
He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|work=BBC Sport|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>
He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Ahead of the [[2017 Toronto Wolfpack season|2017 season]], Bailey signed for newcomers to Rugby League [[Toronto Wolfpack|Toronto]]. He made his début for the new club in an 80-0 victory over [[North Wales Crusaders]]. He then went on to play in seven more games that year and helped in securing the Wolfpack's [[RFL League 1|Kingston Press League 1]] triumph and subsequent promotion to the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].  On 18 January 2018, it was reported that Bailey, along with teammates [[Fuifui Moimoi]] and [[David Taylor (rugby league)|David Taylor]], parted ways with the club by "mutual agreement" following a preseason training camp in [[Portugal]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/toronto-wolfpack-sack-dave-taylor-and-fuifui-moimoi-for-offfield-indiscretions/news-story/f01b434fab3632b02af6c07c53311215 |title=Toronto Wolfpack part ways with Dave Taylor, Fuifui Moimoi reportedly following off-field incident |publisher=[[News.com.au]] |date=19 January 2018 |access-date=19 January 2018}}</ref> It was later reported that the players had twice missed curfew during the trip.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/dave-taylor-fui-fui-moi-moi-reason-sacing-toronto-wolfpack-nrl/1j0yhgo11p7zn1xxuyjn861tm4 |title=REVEALED: The reason Taylor and Moimoi were axed from Toronto |publisher=[[Sporting News]] |date=19 January 2018 |access-date=15 January 2019}}</ref>

===Workington Town===
After his departure from Toronto, Bailey joined league 1 side Workington in January 2018.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/16745069.workington-town-swoop-for-former-super-league-prop-ryan-bailey/ |title=Workington Town swoop for former Super League prop Ryan Bailey |work=[[News and Star]] |date=31 January 2018 |access-date=12 February 2019}}</ref> After playing just six games for the Cumbrian team, Bailey was offered a chance to move up to the Championship to play for Leigh and left Workington in April 2018.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/workington-town/16742382.ryan-bailey-leaves-workington-town-to-join-up-with-leigh-centurions/ |title=Ryan Bailey leaves Workington Town to join up with Leigh Centurions |work=News and Star |date=18 April 2018 |access-date=12 February 2019}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions and retirement===
Bailey joined Leigh in April 2018 and made 11 appearances for the club in the [[2018 RFL Championship|2018 season]].<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leigh-centurions-swoop-six-time-12384761 |title=Leigh Centurions swoop for six-time Grand Final winning prop Ryan Bailey |work=[[Daily Mirror]] |date=18 April 2018 |access-date=12 February 2019}}</ref> In November 2018, Bailey joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a trial contract but decided to retire from the game in February 2019 aged 35.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/ex-leeds-rhinos-star-ryan-bailey-links-up-with-his-old-pal-at-featherstone-rovers-1-9469668 |title=Ex-Leeds Rhinos star Ryan Bailey links up with his old pal at Featherstone Rovers |work=[[Yorkshire Post]] |date=29 November 2018 |access-date=12 February 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47213242 |title=Ryan Bailey: Former Leeds Rhinos prop retires from rugby league |work=[[BBC Sport]] |date=12 February 2019 |access-date=12 February 2019}}</ref>

==Controversies==
In July 2003, he was found guilty of brawling in the street in Leeds, and was sent to a [[Her Majesty's Young Offender Institution|young offender institution]] for nine months.<ref>{{cite news| url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/uk_news/england/west_yorkshire/3104617.stm| work=BBC News| title=Rugby league stars jailed| date=29 July 2003| access-date=23 May 2010}}</ref>

Bailey played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] as a {{rlp|pr}} in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Bradford Bulls]]. He was then selected in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain team]] to compete in the end of season [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] Bailey played from the interchange bench in the Lions' 44–4 loss. On 23 December 2004 the [[Rugby Football League]] fined Bailey along with Leeds' teammate [[Keith Senior]] £1,500 each after they tested positive to the banned stimulant [[ephedrine]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Fisher|first=Michael|title=More education crucial as trio fail drugs test|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2393352/More-education-crucial-as-trio-fail-drugs-test.html|access-date=8 September 2013|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=24 December 2004}}</ref>

Bailey was charged with "evading, refusing or failing to submit to sample collection" during a routine drug testing session at [[Toronto Wolfpack|Toronto]]'s [[Lamport Stadium]] on 30 May 2017. An arbitration panel appointed by the UK National Anti-Doping Panel concluded that Bailey had committed an anti-doping rule violation, but that the exceptional circumstances of the case were such that there was no fault or negligence to be apportioned to Bailey and therefore no suspension under the anti-doping rules should be applied.  The panel heard evidence, including psychiatric evidence, over three days and heard that Bailey had during the testing process, developed a genuine, if unjustified, belief that the water offered to him by the testing officials was contaminated in some way.<ref name=BBC030118>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42558028 |title=Ryan Bailey: Ex-England & Great Britain prop avoids ban after 'landmark' drugs case |work=[[BBC Sport]] |date=3 January 2018 |access-date=7 January 2018}}</ref>  The panel also noted that Bailey did take a drugs test three days after the original incident that was negative and stressed that they did not consider Bailey a cheat or someone trying to cover up drug taking.<ref name=BBC030118/>  The case is believed to be the first case where a player who has refused to take a test has not been punished.<ref name=BBC030118/>
